novel_features.name: Factory-confidence rank + label
novel_features.description: Rank wholesale offers by how likely the seller is the real factory, not a reseller, and label each trader / likely-factory / verified-factory.
novel_features.rationale: Combines factory-inspection flags, the 深度验厂 service tag, reorder rate, and supplier trade scores in one local ranking — signals that exist per-offer but no scraper ranks together.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when an agent must pick a manufacturer over a middleman among dozens of near-identical listings.

novel_features.name: Repurchase-rate leaderboard
novel_features.description: Rank synced offers and suppliers by 回头率 (buyer reorder rate), with a minimum-transaction floor to suppress low-volume noise.
novel_features.rationale: Reorder rate is the most trust-predictive 1688 field; competitors expose it raw but never as a first-class ranking, and it only means something once enough transactions back it.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use it to surface suppliers buyers actually come back to, instead of trusting a one-off star rating.

novel_features.name: Price / repurchase / transaction drift
novel_features.description: Show how an offer's price, reorder rate, and 30-day transaction count moved across your stored snapshots.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a local snapshot history keyed by synced_at that only a persistent store has; every competing scraper is stateless and can never answer 'did this move since last week?'.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this before a reorder to see whether a 'limited-time' price actually dropped or a supplier's reliability is trending down.

novel_features.name: Cross-supplier SKU compare
novel_features.description: Render a side-by-side table of price, MOQ, tier, reorder rate, transactions, factory flags, and trade scores for several offers of the same product.
novel_features.rationale: Joins offers and suppliers in the local store across N offer IDs; no stateless scraper does cross-offer joins.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use it to make the final buy decision between a handful of shortlisted suppliers in one view.

novel_features.name: Supplier reliability rollup
novel_features.description: Aggregate one shop across all its stored offers: trade-service scores, average reorder rate, total transactions, verification badges, offer count, and price range.
novel_features.rationale: Rolls up a supplier's full footprint from synced history; the per-offer scraper view never aggregates a shop across its catalog.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to vet or audit a supplier before committing volume, instead of judging from one listing.

novel_features.name: Saved-query watch (poll-once delta)
novel_features.description: Re-run a saved search, store a fresh snapshot, and print only what changed since last run: price and reorder-rate moves plus newly appeared offers and suppliers.
novel_features.rationale: The diff-since-last-run is only possible with persisted prior snapshots; stateless scrapers re-fetch from zero every time.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use it on a schedule to catch new entrants and price moves in a category without re-reading the whole result set.

novel_features.name: Province price-spread
novel_features.description: Group stored offers for a keyword by Chinese province and report min, median, and max price plus transaction count per region.
novel_features.rationale: Aggregates the per-offer province field across the local store to expose cheaper sourcing geographies no single API call surfaces.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to spot whether a product is meaningfully cheaper out of one manufacturing cluster before narrowing suppliers.

narrative.headline: The free, offline, agent-native 1688 wholesale sourcing CLI — keyword search with the signals the web UI buries (回头率 reorder rate, factory-verified status, supplier trade scores, price drift), persisted to a local store no scraper tracks over time.
narrative.value_prop: Search 1688.com's China-domestic wholesale catalog and get structured offers (tiered price, MOQ, supplier, region, transaction volume) for free, with no paid scraper API and no API key. Every search persists to a local SQLite store, so `drift` shows how prices and reorder rates moved since last week, `factory-find` ranks real manufacturers above resellers, and `supplier-report` rolls up a shop's full reliability footprint. Read-only sourcing research.

quickstart.comment: health check; confirms the mtop endpoint is reachable, no key needed
live wholesale search (translate English terms to Chinese first for rich results)
persist the result set into the local store to build drift history
rank likely real factories above resellers
after a later sync, see which offers moved on price or reorder rate

troubleshoots.symptom: search returns very few or zero results for an English keyword
troubleshoots.fix: 1688's corpus is Mandarin; translate the term to Simplified Chinese (e.g. 'phone case' -> 手机壳) and retry

troubleshoots.symptom: FAIL_SYS_TOKEN_EMPTY or FAIL_SYS_TOKEN_EXPIRED from the API
troubleshoots.fix: the signed-request token expired; the client re-bootstraps automatically on the next call, so just retry once

troubleshoots.symptom: requests blocked or punished (action=deny) from a cloud/CI host
troubleshoots.fix: Alibaba blocks data-center IPs (cloud_ip_bl); run from a residential IP or a residential proxy, not a cloud egress

troubleshoots.symptom: a transcendence command returns an empty result
troubleshoots.fix: run 'sync <keyword>' first to populate the local store; drift/compare/region-spread read stored data, not the live API

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI for read-only sourcing research on 1688.com: searching the China-domestic wholesale catalog, comparing offers on price/MOQ/reliability, distinguishing factories from resellers, and tracking how prices and reorder rates change over time. It is the right tool when an agent needs structured, scriptable wholesale data without a paid scraper subscription.
anti_triggers: Do not use this CLI for Alibaba.com international listings; that is a different site and API
Do not use it to place orders, add to cart, or contact suppliers; it is strictly read-only
Do not use it for Taobao/Tmall consumer retail listings
Do not use it expecting rich results from English keywords; 1688 is a Mandarin marketplace, translate first

recipes.title: Find verified factories for a product
recipes.explanation: Ranks likely manufacturers above traders using factory flags, reorder rate, and trade scores.

recipes.title: Narrow a verbose search payload for an agent
recipes.explanation: Search responses are large and nested; --select pulls only the fields an agent needs so it does not burn context.

recipes.title: Compare shortlisted suppliers head to head
recipes.explanation: Side-by-side price/MOQ/reorder/factory signals for specific offers you already synced.

recipes.title: Check price and reorder-rate drift before a reorder
recipes.explanation: Diffs the latest stored snapshot against prior ones so you see real movement, not marketing 'limited-time' labels.

recipes.title: Rank suppliers by buyer reorder rate
recipes.explanation: Surfaces shops buyers actually come back to, ignoring low-volume noise.

trigger_phrases: who is the cheapest supplier on 1688 for
